original: Persian polymath Omar Khayyam completes the Jalali calendar, a solar calendar with a precise 33-year intercalation cycle and the basis for the Persian calendar still in use today
Treaty of Paris - Flemish relinquish claims over the County of Zeeland
Tommaso Parentucelli succeeds Eugene IV as Pope Nicholas V

Thirteen Years' War: Delegates of the Prussian Confederation pledge allegiance to the Grand Duke of Lithuania and King of Poland, Casimir IV, who agrees to help in their struggle for independence from the Teutonic Knights
Treaty of Alcáçovas: Portugal cedes the Canary Islands to Castile in exchange for claims in West Africa
Veluwe joins the Union of Utrecht
Dutch and English army led by Maurice of Nassau captures heavily protected city of Breda using a small assault force hidden in a peat barge
Joseph Jenkes receives the 1st patent in North America for making scythes from the General Court of Massachusetts
In his will French Cardinal Mazarin bequeaths his famous library to the Collège des Quatre-Nations, part of the University of Paris (opens 1689) - now the oldest public library in France
Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society begins publication
Treaty of Rastatt signed by Austria and France ending hostilities between them during the War of the Spanish Succession
Spain & Britain sign (1st) Convention of Pardo
First African American Mason in North America initiated in Boston
The British First Fleet arrives at Australian territory of Norfolk Island to found a convict settlement
First college orchestra in the US is founded at Harvard in Cambridge, Massachusetts
Illinois passes the first state vaccination legislation in the US
Jews are expelled from Free city of Lubeck, Germany
Vincenzo Bellini's opera "La Sonnambula" premieres at Teatro Carcano in Milan, Italy
Toronto incorporated with William Lyon Mackenzie as its 1st mayor
Franz Grillparzer's "Weh dem, der Lugt" premieres in Vienna
Provisionary Confederate Congress establishes Confederate Army
Battle of Pea Ridge, Arkansas (Elkhorn Tavern)
Battle of Natural Bridge, Florida
Monarch Milan Obrenovic of Serbia crowns himself king
1st US alternating current power plant starts, Great Barrington, Massachusetts
1st US nurses' magazine, The Nightingale, 1st appears, NYC
England beat Australia to win one of the best cricket series ever, 3-2; Jack Brown hits the fastest 50 in test cricket in 28 mins
1st auto in Detroit, Charles B King rides his "Horseless Carriage"

After a meeting in Indianapolis, USA, a group forms the Social Democratic Party and nominates Eugene Debs as its candidate for President in the forthcoming election (becomes the Socialist Party in 1901)
Permanent US Census office created by legislation passed by Congress
The Japanese fleet bombards Vladivostok, the major Russian port on the Pacific
Cubs sign 3rd baseman Harry Steinfeldt to complete Tinker-Evers-Chance
Heavy storm bursts dike, flooding Vlissingen, Netherlands
Nora Blatch is 1st woman elected to American Society of Civil Engineers

Dutch film distributor Jean Desmet opens his first permanent 
 cinema, the Cinema Parisien in Rotterdam
US naval boat "Cyclops" disappears in Bermuda Triangle
Police in Sunbury, Pennsylvania, issue an edict requiring women to wear skirts at least four inches below the knee
The Natal Indian Congress is resuscitated and reorganised at a meeting in Durban, with Ismail Gora as President
GB Shaw's "Back to Methusaleh III/IV" premieres in NYC
MLB St. Louis Cardinals announce their players will wear numbers on their uniforms
British Labour government cuts military budget
Belgium annexes Eupen, Malmö dy & Sankt Vith
China asks for a seat on the Security council
Turkey & Bulgaria sign friendship treaty
Poland occupies the free city of Danzig (Gdańsk)
Sidney Howard & Paul de Kruif's "Yellowjacket" premieres in NYC
Frank Bartell (Czech), cycles record 80.584 mph in LA
Belgium ends Locarno pact
1st US telecast from an airplane, NYC
Robin (Dick Grayson) debuts as Batman's sidekick in issue 38 of Detective Comics by DC Comics
USAAF begins daylight bombing of Berlin
WWII: British and Canadian sailors boards abandoned German U-boat U-774 seeking Enigma code machine, but are forced to scuttle the sub when efforts to tow it fail
Chinese 38th division occupies Lashio
Dutch resistance fighters kill two, and injure one Nazi officer in an attempt to hijack food supplies at de Woeste Hoeve, Netherlands
Erich Honnecker & Erich Hanke flee Nazis
Federico Garcia Lorca's "La Casa" premieres in Buenos Aires
France recognizes Vietnam statehood within Indo-Chinese federation
XB-45, 1st US 4-engine jet bomber, makes 1st test flight, Muroc, CA
Silly Putty goes on sale in the US
Belgium extends conscription to 24 months
Ghana (formerly Gold Coast) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Farthest radio signal heard (Pioneer IV, 400,000 miles)
President Sukarnoof Indonesia disbands existing parliament and replaces it with a new grouping, half appointed by himself
1st London minicabs introduced
Dutch guilder revalued 4.74%
St Louis vote to build a new downtown stadium for the Cardinals
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
US promise Thailand assistance against communist aggression
Constantine succeeds Paul I as king of Greece
Tom O'Hara runs world record mile (3:56.4)
1st nonstop helicopter crossing of North America, JR Willford
Bruce Taylor hits 105 for NZ v India in 1st Test Cricket innings
Barry Sadler's "Ballad of the Green Berets" becomes #1 (13 weeks)
Former union leader Jimmy Hoffa enters Lewisburg Federal Prison
Joseph Stalin's daughter Svetlana Allilujeva approaches the US Embassy in New Delhi, India, and asks for political asylum
Muhammad Aliis ordered by selective service to be inducted
WACS TV channel 25 in Dawson, GA (PBS) begins broadcasting
A Catholic man is shot dead by British soldiers in Belfast, North Ireland
Explosion at makeshift bomb factory of left-wing Weather Underground terrorist group kills three members in a townhouse at 18 West 11th St in Greenwich Village, NYC
The Beatles release single "Let it Be" in UK
Keswick to Penrith railway officially closes
In an exhibition game with the Pirates, Twins Larry Hisle becomes the 1st designated hitter (he hits 2 HRs & knocks in 7 RBIs)
An unnamed Italian industrialist loses a record $1,920,000 at roulette over 5 hours in Monte Carlo Casino
Algiers Accord: Iran and Iraq announce a settlement of their border dispute.
Ice Dance Championship at Gothenburg won by Pakhomova & Gorshkov (URS)

Brian Clark's stage drama "Whose Life is it Anyway?" starring Tom Conti and Jane Asher, and directed by Michael Lindsay-Hogg, opens at the Mermaid Theatre, London

Princess Theater (Latin Quarter, Cotton Club) opens at 200 W 48th NYC

Susan Birmingham makes then world's loudest recorded human shout (120 dB)
In New Bedford, Massachusetts, woman charges she was gang-raped atop a pool table
US Football League begins its 1st season
Twelve-month-long strike in the British coal industry begins
Atlantis (OV-104) rollout at Palmdale
After losing all appeals, American rock singer David Crosby begins serving 5 year sentence for drug and weapons convictions in Texas state prison
Ken Ludwig's "Lend me a Tenor" premieres in London
USSR's Vega 1 flies by Halley's Comet at 8,889 km
6.8 earthquake hits Ecuador, kills 100
Belgian ferry "Herald of Free Enterprise" sinks; 192 die
18th Easter Seal Telethon raises $35,200,000
3 IRA suspects shot dead in Gibraltar by SAS officers
Julie Krone becomes winningest female jockey (1205 victories)
Yanks beat Mets 6-4 in exhibition game (1st meeting since 1985)
SR-71 sets a transcontinental record, flying 2,404 miles in 1:08:17
Council of the Baltic Sea States is founded
The Michelangelo computer virus begins to take affect
Yankee pitcher Pascual Perez suspended for 1 year due to cocaine
Colin Jackson runs world record 60m hurdles indoor (7.30 sec)
Referendum in Moldova results in the electorate voting against potential reunification with Romania
American Express Travel begins charging for domestic air tickets
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Phoenix, Arizona on KEDJ 106.3/100.3 FM
Matt Beck, an angry lottery accountant kills 4 at Connecticut state lottery

9,000 residents are evacuated from Wagga Wagga, Australia, as the Murrimbidgee River threatens to overflow
9 people die after a plane crashes after being ensnared in power lines in Peru
Microsoft is fined €561M ($731M USD) by the Euro Commission for failing to respect an antitrust settlement to provide options to chose an alternative web browser
Syrian rebels capture Ar-Raqqah, their first major city

NASA's Dawn space probe enters orbit around the dwarf planet Ceres

Seventh Democratic presidential candidates debate hosted by CNN and held in Flint, Michigan
"Highest overdose death rates ever recorded in the US", 142,000 overdoses in 2016-17 period according to US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
American WWII aircraft carrier USS Lexington rediscovered in Australia's Coral Sea, lost during 1942 Battle of the Coral Sea
British health officials tells the country it's time "to get on a diet" and urging manufacturers to reduce calories in foods
Nashville mayor Megan Barry resigns a month after admitting to an extramarital affair with her bodyguard
Sri Lanka imposes first nationwide state of emergency since civil war after unrest between Sinhalese and Muslim areas in Kandy District
World's oldest message in a bottle is found in Western Australia, thrown from the German ship Paula 132 years ago on June 12, 1886

American rapper Lil Uzi Vert second album, "Eternal Atake", 
 tops the Billboard charts upon its release

Spacecraft Athena, carrying a NASA ice drill, built by Intuitive Machines, Texas, lands on its side on the Moon, ending its mission
Portuguese navigatorFerdinand Magellanis the first European to sight Guam in the western Pacific
PresidentAndrew Jacksonnominates John McLean to become an associate justice of the US Supreme Court
Edgar Allan Poecourt-martialed and dismissed from West Point military academy for gross neglect of duty and disobedience of orders
Volume One ofThomas Carlyle's famous work "The French Revolution: A History" is accidentally burnt by a maid using it as fire starter before its publication. Carlyle re-writes it.

Giuseppe Verdi's opera "La Traviata" premieres at Teatro La Fenice in Venice, Italy

In his 10th title defense, WBA light welterweight champion Antonio Cervantes of Colombia is beaten by 17-year-old Puerto Rican boxerWilfred Benítezin a 15-round split decision in San Juan, Puerto Rico; Benitez youngest ever world champion
Hustler publisherLarry Flyntand his lawyer shot by a militant white supremacist sniper in Georgia, leaving Flynt crippled and wheelchair bound

First "Lethal Weapon" film starringMel GibsonandDanny Glover, directed by Richard Donner is released
Following Iraq's capitulation in the Persian Gulf conflict, US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushtold Congress that "aggression is defeated. The war is over"
Picasso's painting Tête de Femme is stolen from a London gallery, it is recovered a week later
